volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Persian (Section 1.1.1 - Section 1.1.46b)

sections.label: Section 1.1.1
sections.original: Qui amáns egens ingressus est princeps in Amoris vias ,
sections.translation: He who , falling in love , destitute of means , has first entered upon the paths of love , has in his own labours exceeded all the labours of Hercules . For with the lion [*] , and with the Hydra , with the stag , with the Aetolian boar , with the birds of Stamphalus , with Antaeus , would I rather contend

sections.label: Section 1.1.5
sections.original: quam cúm Amore : ita fio miser quaerendo argento mutuo ,
sections.translation: than with love . So wretched am I become with hunting after money to borrow ; and yet , those whom I ask know of nothing to answer me , except I have got none .

sections.label: Section 1.1.7
sections.original: Qui eró suo servíre volt bene sérvos servitútem ,
sections.translation: The servant that is desirous faithfully to serve his master , i ’ troth , it surely does behove him to treasure up full many a thing in his breast which he may think will please his master , both present and abroad .

sections.label: Section 1.1.10
sections.original: ego neque lubenter servio neque satis sum ero ex sententia ,
sections.translation: I neither serve with cheerfulness , nor am I quite to my master ’ s satisfaction ; but , as though from a running eye , my master is still unable to keep his hands off me , in giving me his commands , in making me the support of his affairs .

sections.label: Section 1.1.13
sections.original: Quís illic est qui cóntra me astat ?
sections.translation: Who ’ s this that ’ s standing opposite to me ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.13b
sections.original: Quís hic est qui sic cóntra me astat ?
sections.translation: Who ’ s that that ’ s standing opposite to me ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.14
sections.translation: It ’ s like Sagaristio .

sections.label: Section 1.1.14b
sections.translation: Surely this is my friend Toxilus .

sections.label: Section 1.1.15
sections.translation: Certainly it is he .

sections.label: Section 1.1.15b
sections.translation: I think it is he .

sections.label: Section 1.1.15c
sections.translation: I ’ ll go meet him .

sections.label: Section 1.1.15d
sections.translation: I ’ ll go up and accost him .

sections.label: Section 1.1.16
sections.translation: O Sagaristio , may the Gods bless you .

sections.label: Section 1.1.16b
sections.original: O Toxilé , dabunt di quae exoptes .
sections.translation: O Sagaristio , the Gods grant you what you may desire . How fare you ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.17b
sections.translation: Just as I can .

sections.label: Section 1.1.17c
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.17d
sections.translation: I still live .

sections.label: Section 1.1.18
sections.translation: Quite then to your satisfaction ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.18b
sections.translation: If the things come to pass which I desire , quite .

sections.label: Section 1.1.19
sections.translation: You deal with your friends in a very silly fashion .

sections.label: Section 1.1.19b
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.19c
sections.translation: Because you ought to give them your commands .

sections.label: Section 1.1.20
sections.original: Mihi quidem tu iám eras mortuos , quia non te visitabam .
sections.translation: As for myself , you were already dead to me , because I haven ’ t seen you .

sections.label: Section 1.1.21
sections.translation: Business , upon my faith —

sections.label: Section 1.1.21b
sections.translation: In the iron chain line , perhaps .

sections.label: Section 1.1.21c
sections.translation: For more than a twelvemonth I ’ ve been promoted in chains to be commanding officer in the basting line [*] at the mill .

sections.label: Section 1.1.23
sections.translation: Why , that ’ s your old line of service .

sections.label: Section 1.1.23b
sections.translation: Have you been quite well all along ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.23c
sections.translation: Not very .

sections.label: Section 1.1.24
sections.translation: I ’ faith , it ’ s with reason then you are so pale .

sections.label: Section 1.1.24b
sections.original: Saucius factus sum in Veneris proelio :
sections.translation: I ’ ve been wounded in the battles of Venus ;

sections.label: Section 1.1.25
sections.translation: Cupid has pierced my heart with his arrow .

sections.label: Section 1.1.25b
sections.translation: Do servants then fall in love here ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.26
sections.original: Quíd ego faciam ? disne advorser ? quasi Titani cum eis belligerem
sections.translation: Why , what could I do ? Was I to be struggling against the Gods ? Was I , like the sons of Titan , to be waging war with the Deities , with whom I am not quite able to cope ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.28
sections.original: Vide modo , ulmeae catapultae tuom ne transfigant latus .
sections.translation: Do you only take care that catapultae made of elm [*] don ’ t pierce your sides .

sections.label: Section 1.1.29
sections.translation: In right royal manner I ’ m celebrating the feast of Freedom [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.1.29a
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.29ab
sections.translation: Because my master ’ s gone abroad .

sections.label: Section 1.1.29ac
sections.translation: Do you say so ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.30
sections.translation: Is he gone abroad ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.30b
sections.translation: If you can bear to be enjoying yourself , do you come : you shall live with me ; you shall be treated with right royal entertainment .

sections.label: Section 1.1.32
sections.original: Váh , iam scapulae pruriunt , quia te ístaec audivi loqui .
sections.translation: Out upon it ( rubbing himself .) my shoulder-blades are quite itching now , because I ’ ve heard you mention these things .

sections.label: Section 1.1.33
sections.translation: But this one thing is torturing me .

sections.label: Section 1.1.33b
sections.translation: Why , what is it ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.34
sections.original: Haéc dies summa hódie est , mea amíca sitne líbera ,
sections.translation: This day is the very last day , to determine whether my mistress is to be free , or whether she is to endure lasting servitude .

sections.label: Section 1.1.34ab
sections.translation: What , then , do you now desire ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.35
sections.original: Fácere ami cum tibi me potis es sempiternum .
sections.translation: You have it in your power to make me your friend for ever .

sections.label: Section 1.1.35b
sections.translation: In what way ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.36
sections.original: Út mihi des nummos sescentos , quós pro capite illius pendam ,
sections.translation: In lending me six hundred didrachms , for me to pay the same for her freedom , which I will forthwith refund you in the next three or four days . Come , do be good-natured ; give me your help .

sections.label: Section 1.1.39
sections.original: Qua cónfidentia rogare tu á med argentum tantum audes ,
sections.translation: With what assurance , you impudent fellow , do you venture to ask so much money of me ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.40
sections.original: ímpudens ? quin si egomet totus véneam , vix recipi potis est
sections.translation: Why , if I myself were to be sold all in one lot , it ’ s hardly possible for as much to be received as you are asking me for ; for now you are asking for water from a pumice-stone , which is all a-dry itself .

sections.label: Section 1.1.42b
sections.translation: Ought you to be treating me in this fashion ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.42c
sections.translation: What am I to do ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.42d
sections.translation: Do you ask the question ? Beg it on loan from somebody .

sections.label: Section 1.1.43
sections.translation: You do the same as you are asking me .

sections.label: Section 1.1.44
sections.translation: I ’ ve been trying ; I ’ ve found it nowhere .

sections.label: Section 1.1.44b
sections.translation: I really will try , if any one will trust me .

sections.label: Section 1.1.45
sections.translation: Am I then to consider it [*] as a thing in possibility ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.45b
sections.original: Si id domi esset mihi , iam pollicerer :
sections.translation: If I had had it at home , I ’ d promise it at once . This is in my power , to use my best endeavours .

sections.label: Section 1.1.46b
sections.original: Quidquid erit , recipe te ad me .
sections.translation: Whatever it is , come you home to me .
